summary: Fair‑quality soundboard capture of a hot desert show with standout Unbroken Chain, Spanish Jam/Other One and So Many Roads, but mixed performance due to Jerry’s health and occasional hissy mix.
review: The recording is a soundboard tape that suffers from noticeable hiss and low vocals, yet remains listenable. Musically the concert shines in its second‑set flow – Unbroken Chain, Eyes of the World, Spanish Jam into The Other One, Days Between and So Many Roads are repeatedly praised – while the first set feels uneven and songs like Liberty draw criticism. Overall fans are divided, celebrating the memorable jams but lamenting Jerry’s waning stamina and the hot venue conditions.

ai_rating.rationale: Over two dozen reviews give an average of ~3.3 stars; many highlight superb jams and classic songs (show strength) while noting hissy board and Jerry’s declining performance (recording/show weaknesses). The balance yields a solid mid‑range rating with high confidence due to volume of consistent feedback.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Board is hissy like a snake; vocals are low, guitars sometimes buried, drums and Phil’s bass are prominent. Overall clarity is acceptable but the mix is not pristine.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as uneven and sometimes a “dud,” but the second set is repeatedly called a gift, with a strong Unbroken Chain → Eyes transition leading into a superb Spanish Jam/Other One sequence and a soaring Days Between to close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed remarks – some note his guitar tone sounded great while others point out weak vocals, health‑related fatigue and moments where he seemed ready to collapse.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is audible even when the mix is poor; a few reviewers mention low vocal levels affecting his presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: Consistently praised for tight grooves (Kreutzmann/Hart) and the “drum circle” vibe; Carter Beauford’s guest spot was criticized as out of sync.
